Success Architects (SAs) are some of our most senior technical experts who provide proactive and reactive Architecture as well as Product Technical expertise. SAs deliver advisory engagements to help maximize customer success and adoption, as well as minimize attrition risk. They partner with key stakeholders at our largest, most complex customers to share their technical thought leadership and inspire the next generation of Digital Marketing Teams. This is an Individual contributor (IC) role. **Responsibilities** Be a trusted Marketing Cloud Subject Matter Expert for the broader Success Architect organisation Engage with our Signature and Strategic customers to evaluate and recommend optimisation strategies for technical architecture, DevOps, performance, and solution design specific to Marketing Cloud Translate complex technical concepts for audiences ranging from Technical, Business to Executive Stakeholders Identify and evaluate capability gaps for standard product capability or identify creative solutions through customisation Facilitate and influence Executive stakeholders while aligning technology strategy to business value and ROI Develop and run playbooks aligned with our Success Architect engagement catalog, tailored to the unique needs and opportunities of Marketing Cloud customers Build strong relationships with both internal and external business partners, contributing to broader goals and growth **Impact of the Role** As a Marketing Cloud Success Architect, you will have a significant impact on our customers' success and the growth of our organization. Your expertise and guidance will directly influence the technical architecture, performance optimization, and solution design strategies for our strategic customers, ensuring their success in leveraging Marketing Cloud's powerful capabilities. By driving customer satisfaction and delivering exceptional value, you will contribute to the overall growth and reputation of Salesforce as a leader in the industry. **Collaboration and Teamwork** Collaboration is at the core of our success, and as a Marketing Cloud Success Architect, you will have the opportunity to work closely with diverse teams of professionals. You will collaborate with customers, colleagues, and partners to evaluate technical architecture, optimize performance, and design effective solutions. By fostering strong relationships and working collaboratively, you will contribute to the collective success of our teams and the achievement of our goals. **Basic Requirements** * 5\+ years proven experience in enterprise consulting, including implementing enterprise software solutions in the Marketing/MarTech space * Experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud Engagement (Email/Mobile) AND one of: Marketing Cloud Personalization OR Marketing Cloud Intelligence OR Marketing Cloud Account Engagement OR similar product * Deep understanding of data modeling, integration architectures, and data governance best practices. * Knowledge of Marketing Cloud Engagement scripting languages (AmpScript, SSJS, GTL, WSProxy) * Experience with system architecture design and integration patterns * Knowledge of Marketing Cloud Engagement Security capabilities and best practices * Excellent communication skills, and ability to work collaboratively with cross\-functional teams from Developer to Executive * Stay up\-to\-date with emerging marketing, data\-related and AI technologies * Proactive and self\-starting attitude with the ability to manage tasks independently while collaborating remotely with customers and colleagues * Values the importance of Data Ethics and Privacy by ensuring that customer solutions adhere to relevant regulations and best practices in data security and privacy **Preferred Requirements** * Experience implementing Salesforce Clouds, Multi\-cloud scenarios \- Sales, Service, Industries, Marketing or Commerce * Salesforce certifications * Familiarity with Salesforce’s technical architecture: APIs, Standard and Custom Objects, APEX * Familiar with Marketing / Personalization platforms * Familiar with AI concepts * Knowledge of Data Management principles and experience with Customer Data Platforms or Data Lakes * Understanding of development tools including SLDS, XML, HTML, SQL, Javascript, JSON, and CSS. * Other business level language skills (e.g. Portuguese, Spanish, Italian, French etc) Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Marketing Executive
description: About SnapTrip \- SnapTrip is an AI\-powered travel platform on a mission to make every trip smarter, more personal, and effortlessly planned. We're early\-stage, backed by a Local Enterprise Office DLR Feasibility Grant, and part of the Slowey MVB Tech Accelerator. We're not building another travel aggregator — we're building the travel co\-pilot people actually want. Now we need the right person to make the world aware of it. The Role \- You'll be our first dedicated marketing hire — which means you'll have the keys to the brand and the freedom to shape it. This isn't a "execute someone else's playbook" role. This is a "write the playbook, then execute it" role. You'll work directly with the founder and be at the heart of every growth conversation. Brand \& content \- * Own SnapTrip's brand voice, visual identity, and tone of voice across all channels * Create compelling content — blog posts, social copy, newsletters, landing pages — that actually converts * Build a consistent content calendar and ensure the brand shows up well everywhere we exist online * Produce or oversee short\-form video, reels, and travel content for social platforms Growth \& performance \- * Plan, run, and optimise digital marketing campaigns across Google, Meta, TikTok, and email * Lead SEO strategy and execution — keyword research, on\-page optimisation, and link building * Own our analytics dashboards — track CAC, retention, and engagement; report insights directly to the founder * Run A/B tests on messaging, landing pages, and ad creatives to continuously improve performance Community \& partnerships \- * Identify and activate partnerships with travel creators, influencers, and micro\-communities * Build and grow our online community — engage users, collect feedback, and turn early adopters into advocates * Support the launch and localisation of SnapTrip's country guides and destination content * Manage PR touchpoints, press releases, and media outreach as we grow our public profile Strategy \& product marketing \- * Develop go\-to\-market strategy for new features and destination launches * Define target personas, refine our positioning, and sharpen our competitive messaging * Collaborate with the founder on pitch decks, investor\-facing materials, and growth narratives * Bring proactive ideas to the table — we want someone who sees opportunity and acts on it What we're looking for \- * 3–5 years in marketing, growth, or brand — ideally in a startup or digital\-first environment * Hands\-on with social, SEO, email, paid ads, and content — not just managing agencies * Comfortable with Canva, Google Analytics, Meta Ads Manager, Mailchimp / Klaviyo, and similar * Self\-starter. Comfortable with ambiguity. Ownership\-oriented. Moves fast without breaking trust Bonus points if you... * Have a genuine love for travel — you get the audience because you are the audience * Are curious about AI tools and how they can supercharge a lean marketing operation * Have grown a personal brand, blog, or community of your own * Have worked at an early\-stage startup before and know what "wearing multiple hats" actually means Pay: From €6,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Flexitime * Food allowance * Profit sharing * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Meat Processing Operatives
description: Fairgreen Products Ltd trading as Noor Foods Naas is seeking four Meat Processing Operatives to join our food production facility in Naas, Co. Kildare. This is an excellent opportunity for reliable and motivated individuals seeking long\-term employment within an established food manufacturing business. Full training will be provided. **Key Responsibilities:** * Preparation, cutting, trimming, deboning, and portioning of meat products * Operating food processing machinery safely and efficiently * Packing, weighing, and labelling products according to food safety regulations * Maintaining strict hygiene and HACCP standards * Carrying out quality control checks * Assisting with production line operations and stock handling * Working as part of a team to achieve daily production targets **Requirements:** * Previous experience in meat processing or food production is desirable but not essential * Ability to work in a chilled production environment * Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality * Reliable, punctual, and capable of following instructions * Good communication and teamwork skills * Flexibility to work shifts and overtime **Benefits:** * €34,000 per annum (full\-time permanent) * Full training provided * Career progression opportunities * Supportive, inclusive workplace **Work Eligibility:** Applicants must be legally entitled to work in Ireland. Non\-EEA nationals must hold a valid employment permit. **How to Apply:** Submit CV to naasnoorfoods@gmail.com Pay: €34,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: Mechanical & Electrical (M&E) Coordinator
description: 12\-month Contract Cental is a global supplier of fully designed, engineered, built and integrated modular and skid\-mounted solutions for the Data Centre, Utilities, Life Sciences, and Oil \& Gas markets. From our headquarters in Carlow, Ireland, we deliver comprehensive solutions for clients worldwide. Our people are the foundation of our success. With three manufacturing facilities and a talented team of engineers, designers, project managers, and tradespeople, our team manages every detail, ensuring seamless delivery from initial design to final installation. We value teamwork, respect, and continuous improvement, creating a collaborative, innovative environment where everyone can reach their potential. We also believe in celebrating achievements and recognising effort. From monthly quality and safety initiatives for breakfast treats and local tool vouchers, to company breakfasts, staff BBQs, and occasional outings, we bring people together to enjoy achievements and strengthen our team spirit. Growth and learning are central to Cental. Whether you’re joining as an apprentice, graduate, or experienced professional, we provide training and development opportunities to help you build a rewarding, long\-term career. If you’re passionate about excellence, innovation, and teamwork, join us and be part of a company that’s engineering solutions and building futures. We are seeking an experienced Mechanical \& Electrical (M\&E) Coordinator to join a data centre project team on a 12\-month contract. The successful candidate will play a key role in coordinating M\&E services across the project lifecycle, ensuring delivery aligns with programme, quality standards, and safety requirements. **Key Responsibilities:** ------------------------- * Coordinate and manage all MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing) aspects of data centre construction projects * Liaise with design teams, subcontractors, and project stakeholders to ensure seamless integration of M\&E services * Review technical drawings, specifications, and design documentation to identify potential issues or clashes * Monitor installation works to ensure compliance with project specifications, industry standards, and health \& safety requirements * Track project progress and provide regular updates to project managers and senior stakeholders * Attend and contribute to project meetings, including coordination and progress meetings * Ensure quality control throughout installation and commissioning phases * Support commissioning activities and handover documentation processes * Assist in resolving technical issues on site in a timely manner **Key Requirements:** --------------------- * Proven experience as an M\&E Coordinator * Strong knowledge of mechanical and electrical systems in large\-scale construction or manufacturing environments * Experience working on complex MEP installations and coordinating multiple subcontractors * Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and specifications * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Strong organisational skills and ability to manage deadlines in a fast\-paced environment * Familiarity with project management tools and reporting processes ##### **What We Offer** * Competitive Salary * Opportunity to work on a high\-profile data centre project * Collaborative and dynamic project environment * Half\-Day Fridays * Bike\-to\-Work Scheme * Free Parking If you are passionate about M\&E and would like to be part of a team that’s transforming modular construction through innovation and high\-quality design, we encourage you to apply.

title: Mechanical Commissioning Technician
description: LotusWorks is an Engineering Services provider specializing in managing Commissioning, Construction Services, Calibration, Operations \& Maintenance scopes of work within global manufacturing facilities. LotusWorks operates globally across EMEA and North America partnering with the world's leading manufacturers across the Semiconductor, Pharmaceutical \& Biologics, Medical Device and Data Centre sectors. Our team of Engineering and Technical professionals is immersed in cutting\-edge technologies and innovations. LotusWorks is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, integral to our people\-first approach. As a Mechanical Commissioning Technician working with the LotusWorks team, you will be based on a client site within the Semiconductor sector at a state\-of\-the\-art facility in Leixlip, Co. Kildare. You will be responsible for both decommissioning and commissioning of facilities equipment across utility and process systems, supporting the safe start\-up, testing, and handover of mechanical systems in a live, high\-tech manufacturing environment. You will be responsible for the mechanical connection and installation of process tools, supporting tool start‑up activities during a semiconductor facility ramp‑up. The role operates in a fast‑paced, schedule‑driven environment and requires close coordination with multiple trades and project teams. ### **Responsibilities:** * This position is responsible for the commissioning and de\-commissioning of a range of Mechanical associated Live Systems, including but not limited to those listed below: * + Air Handling Units (AHUs) + Chillers and Boilers + Compressed Air Systems + Scrubbed Exhaust and General Exhaust Systems + Water Treatment Systems + Industrial Waste Systems (IWS) + Process equipment and associated mechanical services + Tool hook‑up and turn‑on readiness activities + Associated piping, fittings, and mechanical interfaces * Work as a Mechanical Technician as part of a wider multi\-disciplinary team supporting commissioning and de\-commissioning activities. * Demonstrate ownership and initiative in a high‑paced, schedule‑driven environment. * Deliver assigned tasks in line with project milestones and safety requirements. * Carry out all duties in a manner that poses no risk to personnel safety or facility operations. * Work with trades to ensure equipment is installed in a safe, compliant, and approved manner. * Follow detailed installation and tool turn‑on documentation. * Monitor contractor installation workmanship to ensure compliance with specifications. * Track mechanical installation activities against the project schedule. * Support tool start‑up activities, including inspections, pressure testing, and verification prior to handover. * Witness tests and inspections carried out on mechanical systems and connections. * Generate Before Action Reviews (BARs) and execute documentation to support decommissioning activities on live systems in line with site procedures. * Generate and execute commissioning documentation including OATs and FATs (Operational and Functional Acceptance Tests). * Work with Operations and Engineering teams to develop testing protocols and system start‑up procedures. * Commission mechanical equipment including pumps, fans, motors, compressors, boilers, chillers, and air handlers. * Troubleshoot and resolve system and equipment issues safely and efficiently. * Work to tight timelines while maintaining strict safety and quality standards * Carry out QA/QC walk\-downs of mechanical systems and equipment. * Ensure documentation complies with client quality and commissioning requirements. * Generate, execute, and maintain commissioning documentation in a controlled and organised manner. * Record test results and system performance data. * Support the closure of identified field issues and verify that all corrective actions have been effectively implemented * Interact and respond to Project and Commissioning Leads direction. * Attend daily meetings and provide accurate status updates on assigned systems and activities. * Co\-ordinate with commissioning, operations, engineering, and safety teams to support system readiness. * Communicate clearly and professionally with peers, supervisors, and client stakeholders. * Execute all works in strict accordance with site safety protocols, procedures, permits, and approved documentation. * The role involves the commissioning and de\-commissioning of multiple Live Systems, executed with unwavering adherence to COHE (Control of Hazardous Energy) procedures to ensure the highest standards of safety and operational control. * Maintain exceptional attention to safety standards, particularly when working on live monitoring and FMS systems. * Identify, report, and mitigate risks associated with Mechanical commissioning \& de\-commissioning activities. * Any other duties or responsibilities that may be assigned to you in your role. ### **Skills Required:** * Ideally a minimum of 2 years' industry experience with a mechanical maintenance and /or start up mechanical process, Installation or commissioning environments. * Strong working knowledge of industrial HVAC and refrigeration systems, including fans, pumps, and associated equipment. * Excellent troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment. * Proficient in Microsoft 365 suite applications, with the ability to produce accurate documentation and support operational tasks. * Strong communication, documentation, and interpersonal skills. * Flexibility and accountability in approach to work. ### **Education / Licenses / Certifications:** * Time\-served mechanical trades qualification or a Degree in Mechanical Engineering with relevant experience. * Proof of education, licenses, and certifications will be required where applicable ### **Benefits:** * VHI Healthcare Cover * Life Assurance * Pension Contribution * Training And Educational Assistance * Annual GP Check\-Up * Extra Annual Leave * Healthcare Screening * Recognition Rewards \#LI\-DNP *LotusWorks is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees feel valued, respected, and empowered.*

title: NetOps Engineer - Test
description: **Company Description** Arista Networks is an industry leader in data\-driven, client\-to\-cloud networking for large data center, campus and routing environments. What sets us apart is our relentless pursuit of innovation. We leverage the latest advancements in cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and software\-defined networking to provide our clients with a competitive edge in an increasingly interconnected world. Our solutions are designed to not only meet the current demands of the digital landscape but to also anticipate and adapt to future challenges. At Arista we value the diversity of thought and perspectives that each employee brings to the table. We believe that fostering an inclusive environment, where individuals from various backgrounds and experiences feel welcome, is essential for driving creativity and innovation. Our commitment to excellence has earned us several prestigious awards, such as Best Engineering Team, Best Company for Diversity, Compensation, and Work\-Life Balance. At Arista, we take pride in our track record of success and strive to maintain the highest standards of quality and performance in everything we do. **Job Description** **Who You'll Work With** Arista Networks is looking for world\-class Software Test Engineers to help us build our next\-generation network management system. The role requires a broad knowledge of computer network design, configuration, and monitoring in various environments (e.g. data\-center, enterprise, and service provider). You will be part of a design and test team that works on the latest technologies in state streaming, big data, and advanced analytics. The goal is to help network operation teams effectively manage their large networks that deviate away from their antiquated, time\-consuming approaches. Our solution will instead help our customers overcome many of their challenges through our provisioning and telemetric solutions, for both on\-premise and cloud solutions. **What You'll Do** * Designing and executing system\-level test cases for our next\-generation management system. This will involve both scale and performance\-related test cases to observe how components within our system behave in the most strenuous environments. * Working alongside the development team to help root\-cause any issues found. This will involve running many experiments to prove/disprove any current theories, often involving automation tools to assist you with your task. * Writing up detailed bug reports that include a complete characterization of the system at time of failure, while describing the impact that it can have on our customers. * Working with our Technical Support team to help reproduce, debug, and resolve any issues reported by our customers. * Strong passion for beautiful and intuitive user\-interfaces (GUIs and APIs) that create a pleasurable user\-experience (UX) for our customers. **Qualifications** * A BSCS/CE degree plus 3\+ years of experience or MS degree in telecommunications, computer networking, or related field. * Experience in designing, deploying, managing, and troubleshooting IP and Ethernet networks. * Experience in 3 or more of the following networking protocols and technologies (e.g. BGP, OSPF, ISIS, VRRP, LACP, STP, IP Multicast, ECMP, Port\-Mirroring, QoS, VXLAN, MPLS, EVPN, etc) * Experience with Linux operating system and its popular technologies (e.g. KVM, Docker, Kubernetes, Hadoop, Hbase) * Some familiarity with scripting languages such as Python or TCL. * Experience with provisioning and deployment frameworks (e.g. Ansible, Puppet, Chef) a plus. * Strong communication skills. * CCIE certification a plus.

title: Network Service Engineer
description: **Job Description** **Your role** The Network Service engineer is responsible for monitoring and maintaining Digital Realty's Interconnectivity Network infrastructure to ensure minimum network downtime and customer service impacts. Troubleshoot all network incidents and any customer reported services issue within agreed upon SLA. If needed, the engineer is responsible for coordinating with internal and external providers to troubleshoot and fix any network/customer service issues. **What You’ll Do** * Real\-Time Monitoring: Proactively tracking network health, traffic, and performance metrics to prevent failures. * Trouble Ticket Support: Create and manage trouble tickets with our equipment vendors and our fiber providers and support customer facing trouble tickets (Cloud Connect, Service Exchange, DRIX, IP Bandwidth, Metro Connect (Dark Fiber), Metro Connect (Ethernet), Metro Connect (Managed Wave), Ports, Links, Connections, Virtual Router). * Incident Management \& Response:Detecting, analyzing, and resolving network outages, outages, or performance degradation. * Maintenance \& Updates: Support the installation, configuring, and updating hardware, and firmware, including patch management. * Reporting: Providing detailed analysis regarding network performance, availability and recommending infrastructure design and routing improvements. * Incident Management, Incident Diagnosis and Incident Resolution within SLA's, OLA's and KPI's, ensuring that all incidents are investigated in a timely manner. * Provide regular updates to ticketing systems while progressing incidents through to resolution. * Liaising and escalating with external suppliers and other Digital Realty departments to ensure minimum disruption and downtime on the network. * Staying up to date with new network technology supported by the NOC. **What You’ll Need** * A degree in telecommunications or equivalent, relevant industry experience. * Experience working with Juniper, Ciena, and Cisco network equipment are a plus. * Professional certifications such as CCNA, JNCIA, or Optical Associate/Foundation are a plus. * Proven experience working within a Network Management or Operations Center environment in the Managed Services (Data or Voice), Telecoms, ISP, or Cloud Service Provider sectors. * Experience working within a Data Center (co\-location) environment is advantageous. * Hands\-on experience with network monitoring tools and trouble\-ticketing systems. * Strong understanding of TCP/IP networking fundamentals. * Proficient in LAN and WAN troubleshooting, including physical layer cabling issues in co\-location environments. * Working knowledge of IP/MPLS networks (experience with Juniper MX Series is desirable). * Working experience of Optical Ethernet and Transport Network. (Ciena 6500 and Waveserver AI desirable) * Working experience with Optical Ethernet and Transport Networks (experience with Ciena 6500 and Waveserver AI is desirable). * Solid understanding of routing principles. * Experience working with VMware and Linux\-based systems. * Familiarity with Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute, AWS Direct Connect, Google Cloud Interconnect, or other CSP connectivity services is an advantage. * Scripting knowledge in Python or experience with Ansible automation tools is a plus. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills and strong attention to detail. **About Us** **A bit about us** Digital Realty brings companies and data together by delivering the full spectrum of data center, colocation and interconnection solutions. PlatformDIGITAL, the company’s global data center platform, provides customers with a secure data meeting place and a proven Pervasive Datacenter Architecture (PDx) solution methodology for powering innovation and efficiently managing Data Gravity challenges. Digital Realty gives its customers access to the connected data communities that matter to them with a global data center footprint of 300\+ facilities in 50\+ metros across 25\+ countries on six continents. To learn more about Digital Realty, please visit digitalrealty.com or follow us on LinkedIn and Twitter . **What We Can Offer You** Our rapidly evolving business sector offers the opportunity to be part of a courageous and passionate team who work together to understand and meet the changing needs of our global customers. Join us and you’ll be part of a supportive and inclusive environment where you can bring your whole self to work. As part of our team, you’ll get to work with people from different business areas, challenge the way we do things and put your ideas into action. We’ll also give you plenty of development opportunities so you can build a rewarding and successful career with us. Apply today, take charge of your career and grow your talents with us. **Safely Powering Progress** Safety isn't just a priority here at Digital; it's critical to everything we do. Safeguarding lives, protecting assets, and securing data aren't just ideals \- they're essential pillars of our commitment to excellence for our people, our partners and our customers. We have a culture of care where every member of Team Digital embraces a relentless pursuit of working safely across Digital Realty. Together we are Safely Powering Progress. **Our Compensation Philosophy** Digital Realty offers its employees a highly competitive compensation package, excellent benefits, and an environment that recognizes and rewards your contributions. Central to our compensation philosophy is rewarding our employees for achieving the values and objectives aligned to the company's overall goals and values.

The EllisDon core values of *Integrity* and *Mutual Respect* welcomes **everyone** , at work and in the community, and our value of *Mutual Accountability,* means that we all have a role to play. As an EllisDon employee, this will ultimately be **your** commitment to Inclusive Diversity. Accommodation for Applicants with disabilities will be made during the recruitment process when requested. We are committed to providing a positive candidate experience and ensuring timely updates are provided to all candidates. If you haven’t already, be sure to create a profile on our Careers page to remain up to date on the status of your application and learn about new career opportunities as they arise. EllisDon uses AI tools to assist in screening and assessing applicants for this position.

title: Operations Administrator
description: **Role Overview** **JOB DESCRIPTION** The role is accountable for the operational processing and control of settlements, fund\-related activity, and client transactions, supporting end‑to‑end transaction lifecycles within a regulated financial services environment. This role requires a flexible and versatile operations professional who can operate across multiple processes, support business continuity through cross‑training, and respond effectively to changing volumes and priorities. The successful candidate will demonstrate strong operational expertise, a client‑centric mindset, and the ability to leverage technology, automation, and AI‑enabled tools to enhance efficiency, reduce risk, and improve data quality in a regulated financial services environment. **Key Objectives** * Ensure timely, accurate settlement of equity, fixed income, and related asset class transactions * Support client transaction lifecycles and fund operations activities, ensuring transparency, accuracy, and strong controls * Proactively manage exceptions, breaks, and settlement risks in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders * Contribute to operational excellence through continuous improvement, automation, and AI‑supported workflows * Maintain high service standards aligned to client expectations, regulatory requirements, and internal policies **Responsibilities** **Key Responsibilities** **Trade Settlement \& Transaction Management** * Process and settle equity, bond, and other market trades in line with market deadlines and internal procedures * Perform daily input, validation, monitoring, and control of executed trades across multiple systems * Ensure all transactions are captured accurately and in line with Transaction Reporting and regulatory requirements * Investigate and resolve settlement failures, mismatches, and exceptions, performing root cause analysis where required **Client Transactions \& Fund Operations Support** * Support the end‑to‑end processing of client transactions, including subscriptions, redemptions, and related fund activity where applicable * Liaise with Front Office, Fund Accounting, Custodians, Brokers, and Transfer Agents to resolve trade, settlement, or fund\-related issues * Maintain strict service levels and responsiveness for client‑driven activity * Provide operational support for fund lifecycle events such as corporate actions, income events, and reconciliations **Controls, Risk \& Regulatory Support** * Ensure adherence to internal policies, operational controls, and escalation frameworks * Support audit, compliance, and regulatory reviews through accurate record‑keeping and timely responses * Escalate unresolved issues or material risks in a timely and proportionate manner **Reporting, Data \& AI Enablement** * Produce daily, weekly, and monthly operational, client, and fund‑related reporting * Use data analytics, automation tools, and AI‑assisted solutions to improve exception management, reconciliations, and reporting accuracy * Participate in initiatives focused on straight‑through processing (STP), data quality improvement, and operational resilience **Continuous Improvement \& Team Contribution** * Identify opportunities for process efficiencies, automation, and quality enhancements * Assist in the development and maintenance of procedures and best‑practice documentation * Support departmental training, onboarding, and knowledge sharing initiatives **Qualifications** **Key Requirements** **Essential** * Minimum 5 years’ experience in settlements, middle office, or operational roles within financial services * Strong understanding of trade lifecycles, operational controls, and post‑trade processing * Excellent attention to detail with strong numerical and analytical capability * Proven ability to prioritise deadlines and manage high‑volume workflows * Strong investigative and problem‑solving skills with a proactive mindset * Team player with strong interpersonal and communication skills * Proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel (reconciliations, analysis, reporting) * Self‑starter, results‑driven, and committed to delivering high‑quality outcomes **Desirable** * Experience supporting fund operations or collective investment structures * Understanding of equities, fixed income, and derivatives products * Exposure to automation, AI tools, or data‑driven operational improvements * Regulatory or audit experience within a controlled financial services environment * Third‑level qualification in Business, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline

title: Pension & Life Administrator
description: **Company Background** Financial Planning Matters specialises in providing independent, tailored financial advice to high\-earning professionals, company directors, and self\-employed individuals seeking to build, grow, and protect their wealth. Based in Sandyford, Dublin, the firm offers strategic solutions such as pension and retirement planning, tax\-efficient profit extraction, investment strategies, and life and business protection. Trusted by over 5,000 clients across Ireland, the company collaborates with leading providers like Zurich, Aviva, Standard Life and Irish Life. Using a fully digital service model, Financial Planning Matters ensures an efficient and accessible experience for clients, helping them achieve long\-term financial security. **The opportunity** A CFP™ qualified, Central Bank of Ireland regulated independent financial planning firm. Managing c. €100m in client assets and growing fast and we looking for a Pension \& Life Administrator to take full ownership of its pension, life, and protection administration. This is a standout role for the right person. You’ll be the operational backbone that keeps advisers advising and clients impeccably served, with real autonomy from day one. The firm is scaling, digitally ambitious, and deliberately small enough that your work is seen and your growth is backed. This is a career move, not just another line on a CV. If you want to be chased and micromanaged, this isn’t for you. If you want ownership, a clear path to grow, and a firm that invests in you, read on. Why this role stands out • A genuine seat at a growing, ambitious practice — not a cog in a large back office. • Real autonomy: you’ll own your pipeline and be trusted to run it. • Full QFA / educational support and study leave. • Direct line to decision\-makers — your initiative shapes how things are done. • A salary that reflects the value of getting this right: €45k–€50k for the right candidate with an annual bonus thats based on the overall company performance. **What you’ll own** • New business processing across pensions (PRSAs, PRBs, ARFs, occupational schemes), life cover, and income protection — submission through to issue. • Provider relationships (Irish Life, Zurich, Aviva, New Ireland, Standard Life) — tracking applications, clearing requirements, resolving queries. • Audit\-ready client records across CRM and provider portals, fully compliant with the Central Bank Consumer Protection Code. • The ongoing servicing pipeline: renewals, fund switches, contribution changes, retirement and death claims. • Commission and trail reconciliation against provider statements. • Adviser file prep ahead of client meetings and reviews. • AML/KYC, GDPR, and Central Bank compliance across everything you touch. **Who we’re looking for** • A self\-starter who uses their initiative and takes ownership without being chased. • A can\-do attitude — solves problems, doesn’t just flag them. • Someone who sees this as a long\-term career step and wants to grow as the firm grows. • Meticulous attention to detail and a genuinely organised, process\-driven mind. • A confident communicator, equally comfortable with providers and clients. **Essential** • 2\+ years’ life and pensions administration, ideally in an Irish brokerage or advisory firm. • QFA qualified, or actively working towards it (APA Pensions and/or Life minimum). • Strong working knowledge of the Irish pension and protection landscape and provider systems. **Desirable** • Experience with Irish brokerage CRM/back\-office systems. • Familiarity with the Consumer Protection Code and Minimum Competency Code. • Exposure to a digital\-first, growing practice. **Ready to back yourself?** This role is for doers. People who take ownership, move things forward, and lift the energy in a room rather than drain it. If you need chasing, hand\-holding, or a reason to do the bare minimum, save us both the time. But if you’re sharp, ambitious, and want a firm that will back you, trust you, and grow with you, we want to hear from you. Show us what you’ve got. Apply now with your CV and a few lines on why this is the move for you.

* To ensure employee’s feedback is communicated upon in a timely manner and assist in taking all necessary action to prevent re occurrences; * To assist in coordinating all Employee Events and constantly improving employee experience; * To maintain an accurate inventory of the employees’ lockers and liaise with the maintenance, accommodation and People \& Culture teams to ensure cleanliness and good maintenance of all locker rooms at all times; * To assist on the marketing side of all aspects i.e. recruitment, training, employee experience, employer branding by promoting internally and externally; To collaborate with the marketing department on finding the best way to do so. * To ensure the employee performance is monitored in the probationary period (1 / 2 / 3 / 6\-month) by liaising with HOD’s . * To support the HOD’s with Grievance and Disciplinary procedures by taking part as a note taker. * To assist the team with any additional tasks or projects. **References:** All employment offers are made subject to us receiving two satisfactory references, which could be: * A corporate email and/or phone number or * A letter in a headed paper signed by the manager / HR We will not ask for reference details until you reach the interview stage. **All Candidates must have the right to work in Ireland** * Excellent Room Employee Rates in over 500 Minor Hotel properties worldwide * Unlimited access to the eLearning platform * Meals whilst on duty in our employee restaurant * Complimentary provision and laundry of uniforms * Employee Recognition Awards * Enhanced Employee Experience in a 5\-Star environment * Employee Assistance Program \- mental health and wellbeing support * Competitive Salary * Career Progression * And more...

title: Private Equity Associate

Strong acumen of Alteryx is a distinct advantage. * FIS Investran or Arcesium or equivalent double entry accounting platform knowledge a distinct advantage. * Prior experience in Private Equity fund administration or alternatives funds industry preferred. **ABOUT US** J.P. title: Product Designer - E-Commerce
description: **About Phorest** Phorest powers over 12,000 salons, spas, and medspas in UK, Ireland, Germany, Australia, Finland, the USA and Canada. The platform supports hundreds of thousands of professionals every day and millions of consumers. Our system touches every point of the salon and medspa experience \- from the in\-salon software appointment calendar and CRM with a fully integrated marketing suite, to staff apps to payments. Over €4 billion was processed in salons and clinics last year using Phorest, and each month 100’s of thousands of online bookings are created. We also provide customers with amazing experiences for their clients through custom native apps for booking appointments and eCommerce solutions to help sell online 24/7\. Why? Because we’re passionate about helping our salons find their dream clients and keep them coming back more often, spending more, and spreading the word. ****Phorest’s E\-Commerce Team**** The E\-Commerce team builds the guest\-facing experiences that connect salons and clinics with their clients 24/7, helping people discover a business, explore services and products, and make a purchase or booking in just a few taps. This Product Design role focuses on B2C with a direct line to salons and clinics guests. What is shipped has an immediate, measurable impact on conversion, retention, and brand loyalty. This team sits at the heart of digital growth for salons. From online booking and discoverability, to product shopping, gift cards, promotions, and loyalty\-driving journeys, they create the moments that turn “I’m browsing” into “I’m booked” or “I’ve bought” and bring guests back again and again. It’s a space where craft matters: performance, trust, accessibility, and delightful interaction design all have a measurable impact. We’re looking for a Product Designer who’s excited by high\-visibility, high\-iteration work and who enjoys blending creativity with strong product thinking. You’ll partner closely with Product and Engineering in a 3 Amigos team to shape the experience vision, validate it with research and data, and deliver customer\-facing features that help salons and clinics reach their guests, tell their story, and build lasting loyalty. ****The Opportunity**** * You will play a critical role in evolving our end\-to\-end consumer shopping experiences that help guests confidently discover, choose, and purchase products. * You will work in a tightly\-knit group alongside a Product Manager and Engineering Manager to shape ideas, define outcomes, and iterate toward the best solution. * You will collaborate closely with engineering to build, test, and ship ideas and to learn from real\-world performance (funnels, experiments, and feedback). * You will balance fast iteration with high craft, ensuring the experience feels trustworthy, accessible, and delightful across platforms. * Your work will directly influence commercial outcomes for salons and clinics, helping them increase their revenue while creating better experiences for their guests. * You will be part of a small group of skilled, driven Product Designers, contributing to shared patterns and the design system so the experience stays consistent across our Products. ****What You'll Do**** * Champion our branded salon app within your team, utilising modern workflows and user\-centric thinking to design impactful solutions that directly address our clients' evolving needs. * Work in a product engineering environment, working closely with an engineering team to ensure what was shipped is meeting customer needs. You’re comfortable talking to customers, testing prototypes, and iterating on designs until you’re confident customers will be happy with your solutions. * Challenge assumptions. While Phorest has a wealth of industry experience to tap into, with fresh eyes you will still want to validate ideas with our customers, many of which are right on our doorstep, or accessible through our very active community. * Plan, run, and synthesise user research to validate assumptions, de\-risk decisions, and improve the experience. * Create wireframes, high\-fidelity UI, and prototypes that clearly communicate intent and enable fast iteration. * Design user\-centred journeys across web and mobile, balancing accessibility, performance, and brand craft. * Promote design best practices and raise the bar through constructive critique, feedback, and continuous improvement. * Drive the evolution of the design system, partnering with Product and Engineering to elevate the patterns into a more modern, scalable framework for the branded app. * Embrace and evolve our way of working. Bring a strong growth mindset to the ever\-changing tech landscape, proactively explore, test, and integrate emerging AI\-assisted workflows and design tools to define new, industry\-standard ways of working efficiently and creatively. ****Who You Are**** * 4\+ years product design experience (UX/UI). * Strong UI skills and a sharp visual design taste, giving you the ability to step into a product with a solid existing foundation and successfully deliver a significant visual and aesthetic uplift. * Strong understanding of information architecture, user experience, interaction, and visual design. * Proficiency in Figma and design prototyping tools and design storytelling. * Ability to apply user research methodologies and data to inform design decisions. * Familiarity with accessibility standards and responsive design. * Driven user\-centric design, through an understanding of user needs, behaviours, and motivations. * Collaboration \& communication skills, with the ability to build good relationships and communicate effectively both verbally and in written format. * Problem\-solving: Able to work autonomously in ambiguous or complex environments, including being comfortable working within technical infrastructure limitations and EU/US compliance frameworks that heavily impact user flows and UX writing. * A can\-do attitude with a growth mindset and eagerness to learn. **Benefits** 🧘 Your wellbeing is important to us \- we provide private healthcare, 2 Wellness Days, an employee assistance program and a free online GP service. 💰 As part of our Financial Wellbeing, we provide competitive Compensation, an Employee Share Purchase Scheme, Pension, Life Assurance, and Income Protection. 🚵🏿 We help you travel by providing a bike to work scheme as well as tax saver transport tickets. 🦸‍♀️ We support the women who work in Phorest by offering 2 weeks leave for Fertility Treatment, Pregnancy Loss and Menopause. 🍼 We care for your family and provide Enhanced Maternity and Paternity Benefits. 🌳 We grow our own timber! We provide a great learning environment and extensive development opportunities. We run development programs and provide access to many online resources including LinkedIn learning. 🏠 Moving house? Phorest employees get 3 moving days. Want to learn more about Phorest? Check out nothingventured.rocks for our blog and Insights on building an evergreen company from the team here at Phorest. **Phorest is an equal opportunity employer.** For this position, flexi\-time and working from home is possible. We are also open to remote work. title: Quality Assurance Technician
description: **Quality Assurance Technician** **Overview of the Role** An exciting opportunity has opened for a driven Quality Assurance Technician to join a dynamic manufacturing environment where innovation, efficiency, and excellence are at the core of everything we do. In this role, you will be instrumental in strengthening quality systems and embedding Lean thinking across daily operations. You will help shape how we work by improving processes, enhancing product quality, and ensuring strong compliance with all relevant regulatory and industry standards. You will take ownership of internal audit activities, lead investigations into non\-conformances, and drive structured root cause analysis to ensure issues are not only resolved but prevented. Working closely with senior quality leadership and cross\-functional teams, you will contribute to the development and rollout of quality strategies, SOPs, and continuous improvement initiatives that elevate performance across the site. A key part of your role will involve working with data, tracking performance metrics, identifying trends, and producing clear insights that support decision\-making. You will also support training and awareness programmes to strengthen a culture of quality throughout the organisation. From inspection processes to product release, you will play a hands\-on role in ensuring standards are consistently met and exceeded. **Role Details** * Full\-time\-Permanent position * **Salary range:** €35,000 – €40,000 DOE * **Work location:** Co. Kildare * **Working Hours:** Monday to Friday, 8am\-5pm, No Weekends * **Benefits:** KPI\-related performance bonus, Retention Bonus, Annual Bonus, Cycle to work Scheme, Company Pension Contribution, Employee Assistance Programme, Additional Leave, Wellness Initiatives **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Lean systems and quality processes to improve manufacturing efficiency, product quality, and regulatory compliance * Lead internal quality audits to ensure adherence to company and regulatory standards * Conduct root cause analysis investigations and implement effective corrective and preventive actions * Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance operational performance and reduce quality issues * Manage non\-conformances and support the effective resolution of customer feedback and quality concerns * Work closely with the Quality Manager and cross\-functional teams to develop and implement quality plans and SOPs * Contribute to the rollout of process improvements aligned with national and international quality standards * Perform data analysis to identify trends, risks, and improvement opportunities * Monitor and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) to track quality and operational performance * Prepare trend reports and insights to support continuous improvement initiatives * Support and deliver quality training and awareness initiatives across teams * Maintain product inspection processes and ensure consistent product release standards **What We Are Looking For** * A third level qualification in Science, Food Science, Engineering, Production, or a related discipline * Exposure to Lean principles, Lean Six Sigma, or structured continuous improvement methodologies would be highly beneficial * Strong analytical mindset with excellent attention to detail * Confident communicator with the ability to work effectively across multiple teams * Proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint * A proactive individual who thrives in a fast\-paced, hands\-on manufacturing environment **Why This Role?** This is a fantastic opportunity to make a real and visible impact. You will be joining a collaborative environment where ideas are welcomed, improvement is encouraged, and your contribution will directly influence quality performance and operational success.

title: Refrigeration Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. support for HVAC systems, including AHUs, chillers, heat pumps, dehumidification systems, ventilation plant, and associated mechanical equipment, in line with industry standards and safety regulations. * Diagnose mechanical and control\-system faults, carrying out corrective actions, component replacements, and adjustments to restore system performance and efficiency. * Recommend suitable replacement parts, upgrade options, and refurbishment solutions to support long\-term asset reliability and energy performance. * Responsibility for the maintenance, repair, and commissioning ofthe full range of HVAC and Refrigeration equipment. * Ensure that all customer facilities and services are made available within the requirements of the contract specification, and ensure assets are operating effectively. **Skills \& Qualifications Required for the role;** * Relevant refrigeration qualification (e.g., F\-Gas Category 1 or equivalent) * Strong technical background in commercial or industrial cooling systems * Minimum 3 years' hands\-on experience working with HVAC or mechanical building services systems in a service, maintenance, or commissioning capacity (essential). * Solid understanding of HVAC engineering, including air handling, ventilation, dehumidification, heat transfer, and system controls. * Proven ability to diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical, and control\-related faults. * Competent in the use of diagnostic instruments for airflow, pressure, moisture content, temperature, and system performance measurement. * Experience working on Industrial refrigeration systems such as stand alone Fridge / Freezers, Incubators, Stability Chambers, and blast freezers * Working in ATEX areas (desirable) * Previous experience of API pharmaceutical Industry (desirable) * Previous experience working on Ammonia refrigeration systems (desirable) **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. title: Register your Interest: Civil & Structural Engineers
description: **Register your Interest – Civil Engineers \& Building Structures** **Location: Dublin, Galway and Cork** **Join Us in Shaping the Future of Healthcare, Education and Critical Infrastructure — Structural Engineering** Join a team delivering some of Ireland’s most important built ‑ environment projects and play a hands ‑ on role in shaping safe, resilient and sustainable healthcare, education and critical ‑ infrastructure assets. We ’ re looking for a technically confident Structural Engineer (7\+ years ’ experience) who excels in Eurocode ‑ based design, structural analysis and on ‑ site problem solving to lead design work across major capital frameworks. If you want to influence large ‑ scale, long ‑ term programmes, work with multidisciplinary teams on high ‑ impact projects, and progress your career through meaningful technical responsibility and chartership support, this is the opportunity to make a real, lasting contribution. **Purpose \& Scope of the Role** **Education** A Tetra Tech company is delivering a significant portfolio of built environment projects most notably in the Healthcare, Education, Housing and Critical infrastructure. We are appointed to deliver a number of major projects for clients including: Health Service Executive on Major Capital Frameworks and Department of Education Large Scale Capital Works Programmes delivering new school projects. These projects are of critical importance in delivering essential healthcare and education capacity both regionally and nationally and are among the largest capital projects planned for delivery in the State. If you are interested in being part of the teams delivering these significant infrastructure projects we’d love to hear from you. We are seeking a Structural Engineer with 7\+ years’ experience to join our growing structural engineering teams based in Dublin, Cork and Galway. The successful candidate will have a hands\-on role in the design and delivery of structural engineering services across a broad and interesting range of projects. **Your Impact in This Position** It’s an exciting time to be working for our Buildings and Infrastructure business as we support our clients in transforming our national healthcare and education infrastructure. With a broad portfolio of long\-term framework agreements in place, we are ideally placed to help our clients deliver on the challenges facing them over the coming years in housing, educational and healthcare facilities around the country. Our Buildings and Infrastructure business employs over 1,000 highly skilled and motivated people working on projects for a significant number of healthcare and education clients across Ireland and the UK. We deliver a full range of end\-to\-end services including using the latest technologies and modelling tools. A personal development plan and a transparent career pathway puts you in the driving seat of your career and you’ll be supported as far as you want to go. A career here is far from ordinary. Here you’re not a number, you are part of the solution. **Core Capabilities** * 7\+ years’ experience working as a structural engineer on a broad range of developments * Experience with Eurocodes, ideally with work experience in Ireland, the UK or Europe. * Ability to develop and nurture relationships with colleagues, Design Team members, Clients and Contractors. * Solid technical and analytical skills with demonstrable problem\-solving abilities. * Experience attending site and carrying out inspections in accordance with BCAR is desirable. * Good IT skills and experience with structural analysis packages (such as Master series, Tedd’s, Robot, etc.); * Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written. * Excellent time management skills. * Interested in continuous professional development and learning. * Full drivers licence desirable and valid work permit to work in Ireland. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Masters or Honours Degree (Level 8\+) in Structural Engineering; * Aspiration to get Chartered with Engineers Ireland/ IStructE in the near future If this opportunity suits your career journey, we’d love to hear from you. If we feel you are a good match, we will be in touch to arrange a competency interview. All applications will be considered. Ready to apply? Please have your CV ready and continue with your application online. **Working Environment** This role is based in Dublin, Cork, Galway or Kilkenny, with hybrid working and agile office arrangements offered as standard. Office attendance will be balanced to meet project and client needs, while supporting flexible working for work‑life balance. Travel to client sites, planning authority meetings and project locations across Ireland will be required on an occasional basis. **Why join Tetra Tech?** Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. We support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are Leading with Science® to solve our clients’ most complex problems. In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list and any submitted will be taken as a gift. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Resident Experience Associate

Leasing Support: Assist the Leasing team with enquiries and viewings. * Enquiries: Greet and assist prospective tenants and to qualify and register enquiries and to book viewing appointments at our properties. * Viewings: Conduct informative and engaging viewings to prospective tenants being well\-equipped to answer any questions regarding the property. * Administrative Support: Working with the leasing team to help maintain accurate and organised records of leases, applications, and tenant correspondence. ‘Stock’ Coordination: Collaborate with the property manager and procurement team to ensure adequate stock of goods and services for smooth property operations. * Inspections: Conduct / support inventories, move\-in and move\-out inspections, documenting property conditions. * Administrative Support: Assist the Property Manager with all other general administrative duties. **Targets and KPIs:** * Work diligently to meet specified targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set by the management team. **Personal Qualities:** * Attention to Detail: Demonstrated focus on quality and management systems will be provided, we require that the applicant be proficient with Microsoft Office and Outlook and have an aptitude for learning new software (essential). **Qualifications (desired):** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, or a related field (desired) * Professional certifications (desired) such as: * The Property Institute (TPI)or IRPM Membership. **Remuneration / Bonuses /Benefits** * Base salary: €35,000 * Bonus: Discretionary Bonus Scheme * Benefits: Private health insurance; Pension Scheme; Cycle to Work Scheme; Company Sick Pay. * 5\-day at the rostered location (primarily at Alta Verde, Blackrock, Dublin, but may be required to work at other locations as required by the business). **Equal Opportunities Statement:** We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to creating an inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplace. We provide equal opportunities for everyone and offer reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have a fair chance during the recruitment process. ### **Company Overview** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sectors. title: Retrofit Building Surveyor
description: **Pay:** €50,000\.00\-€65,000\.00 per year **Role** : Retrofit Building Surveyor ***(If you are not meeting the requirements, the application will be rejected from beginning)*** **About Luca Line Services:** LLS delivers high‑quality retrofit solutions across private and SEAI‑funded programmes. We combine technical rigour with customer‑first delivery to decarbonise homes and buildings across Leinster. **Role overview** : As a Retrofit Building Surveyor you will lead technical surveys and on‑site assessments for private and SEAI‑allocated retrofit works. You will translate complex building physics into clear retrofit roadmaps, ensure compliance with SEAI/NSAI standards, and act as the technical face of LLS to homeowners and stakeholders. **Key responsibilities:** Conduct detailed retrofit surveys (measured surveys, fabric, ventilation, heating, renewables, etc.) and produce clear technical reports and measured drawings. Deliver Home Energy Assessments (HEA) and retrofit roadmaps for SEAI One Stop Shop / WHS projects. Ensure compliance with SEAI DTSS \& QADP, NSAI SR54 and Irish Building Regulations; prepare DEAP inputs and BER advisory reports. **Customer engagement:** explain findings, grant eligibility, and recommended measures to homeowners; manage expectations and secure sign‑off. **Technical knowledge:** resolve technical queries, and participate in project scheduling and QA review. **Support estimating \& sales:** provide technical input to quotes and identify appropriate upsell opportunities. Mentor junior surveyors and contribute to continuous improvement of survey templates, checklists and processes. **Essential qualifications \& experience:** Registered SEAI BER Assessor (domestic) with demonstrable experience delivering HEA/One Stop Shop projects. Minimum 4 years’ retrofit or building surveying experience with hands‑on site assessment work. Proven knowledge of SEAI/NSAI compliance, SR54 guidance, and Irish Building Regulations. Strong customer communication and stakeholder management skills. Full driving licence and willingness to travel within Leinster. **Desirable:** Chartered/degree‑level qualification in Building Surveying or equivalent. BER Assessor training certificate. Experience with airtightness testing and ventilation calculations. Familiarity with DEAP BER software.
